web.title: mlx-community/whisper-tiny-mlx - Hugging Face
web.description: This model was converted to MLX format from tiny. Use with mlx git clone https://github.com/ml-explore/mlx-examples.git cd mlx-examples/whisper/
web.markdown:
# whisper-tiny-mlx

This model was converted to MLX format from [`tiny`](https://huggingface.co/mlx-community/whisper-tiny-mlx).

## Use with mlx

```bash
git clone https://github.com/ml-explore/mlx-examples.git
cd mlx-examples/whisper/
pip install -r requirements.txt

>> import whisper
>> whisper.transcribe("FILE_NAME")
```

Downloads last month1,298

Inference Providers [NEW](https://huggingface.co/docs/inference-providers)

This model isn't deployed by any Inference Provider. [🙋Ask for provider support](https://huggingface.co/spaces/huggingface/InferenceSupport/discussions/new?title=mlx-community/whisper-tiny-mlx&description=React%20to%20this%20comment%20with%20an%20emoji%20to%20vote%20for%20%5Bmlx-community%2Fwhisper-tiny-mlx%5D(%2Fmlx-community%2Fwhisper-tiny-mlx)%20to%20be%20supported%20by%20Inference%20Providers.%0A%0A(optional)%20Which%20providers%20are%20you%20interested%20in%3F%20(Novita%2C%20Hyperbolic%2C%20Together%E2%80%A6)%0A)

## Spaces using mlx-community/whisper-tiny-mlx6

## Collection including mlx-community/whisper-tiny-mlx

## Project description

# Whisper

Speech recognition with Whisper in MLX. Whisper is a set of open source speech
recognition models from OpenAI, ranging from 39 million to 1.5 billion
parameters.\[^1\]

### Setup

Install [`ffmpeg`](https://ffmpeg.org/):

```
# on macOS using Homebrew (https://brew.sh/)
brew install ffmpeg
```

Install the `mlx-whisper` package with:

```
pip install mlx-whisper
```

### Run

#### CLI

At its simplest:

```
mlx_whisper audio_file.mp3
```

This will make a text file `audio_file.txt` with the results.

Use `-f` to specify the output format and `--model` to specify the model. There
are many other supported command line options. To see them all, run
`mlx_whisper -h`.

You can also pipe the audio content of other programs via stdin:

```
some-process | mlx_whisper -
```

The default output file name will be `content.*`. You can specify the name with
the `--output-name` flag.

#### API

Transcribe audio with:

```
import mlx_whisper

text = mlx_whisper.transcribe(speech_file)["text"]
```

The default model is "mlx-community/whisper-tiny". Choose the model by
setting `path_or_hf_repo`. For example:

```
result = mlx_whisper.transcribe(speech_file, path_or_hf_repo="models/large")
```

This will load the model contained in `models/large`. The `path_or_hf_repo` can
also point to an MLX-style Whisper model on the Hugging Face Hub. In this case,
the model will be automatically downloaded. A [collection of pre-converted\\
Whisper\\
models](https://huggingface.co/collections/mlx-community/whisper-663256f9964fbb1177db93dc)
are in the Hugging Face MLX Community.

The `transcribe` function also supports word-level timestamps. You can generate
these with:

```
output = mlx_whisper.transcribe(speech_file, word_timestamps=True)
print(output["segments"][0]["words"])
```

To see more transcription options use:

```
>>> help(mlx_whisper.transcribe)
```

### Converting models

> \[!TIP\]
> Skip the conversion step by using pre-converted checkpoints from the Hugging
> Face Hub. There are a few available in the [MLX\\
> Community](https://huggingface.co/mlx-community) organization.

To convert a model, first clone the MLX Examples repo:

```
git clone https://github.com/ml-explore/mlx-examples.git
```

Then run `convert.py` from `mlx-examples/whisper`. For example, to convert the
`tiny` model use:

```
python convert.py --torch-name-or-path tiny --mlx-path mlx_models/tiny
```

Note you can also convert a local PyTorch checkpoint which is in the original
OpenAI format.

To generate a 4-bit quantized model, use `-q`. For a full list of options:

```
python convert.py --help
```

By default, the conversion script will make the directory `mlx_models`
and save the converted `weights.npz` and `config.json` there.

Each time it is run, `convert.py` will overwrite any model in the provided
path. To save different models, make sure to set `--mlx-path` to a unique
directory for each converted model. For example:

```
model="tiny"
python convert.py --torch-name-or-path ${model} --mlx-path mlx_models/${model}_fp16
python convert.py --torch-name-or-path ${model} --dtype float32 --mlx-path mlx_models/${model}_fp32
python convert.py --torch-name-or-path ${model} -q --q_bits 4 --mlx-path mlx_models/${model}_quantized_4bits
```

\[^1\]: Refer to the [arXiv paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.04356), [blog post](https://openai.com/research/whisper), and [code](https://github.com/openai/whisper) for more details.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 ") | 2 months agoMar 31, 2026 | | [.pre-commit-config.yaml](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/blob/main/.pre-commit-config.yaml ".pre-commit-config.yaml") | [.pre-commit-config.yaml](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/blob/main/.pre-commit-config.yaml ".pre-commit-config.yaml") | [feat: fork whisperx to use mlx](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/commit/5e28692c29574ad45453b5a8e610920a21d87c31 "feat: fork whisperx to use mlx Full rework.") | 2 months agoMar 23, 2026 | | [.python-version](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/blob/main/.python-version ".python-version") | [.python-version](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/blob/main/.python-version ".python-version") | [build: upgrade PyTorch to 2.7.1 with CUDA 12.8 and multi-platform sup…](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/commit/95fecb91c8739222a77dd3125f47b7dd6bb98017 "build: upgrade PyTorch to 2.7.1 with CUDA 12.8 and multi-platform support - feat: upgrade PyTorch to 2.7.1 and CUDA 12.8 * Update README setup to require CUDA toolkit 12.8 instead of 12.4 (Linux and Windows) * Bump torch dependency from 2.6.0 to 2.7.1 * Switch the PyTorch CUDA wheel index from cu124 to cu128 - Revert \"docs: add troubleshooting section for libcudnn dependencies in README\" * The issue of relying on two different versions of CUDNN in this project has been resolved. - build(pyproject): relax python version and constrain package deps * Only download torch from PyTorch; obtain all other packages from PyPI. * Restrict numpy, onnxruntime, pandas to be compatible with Python 3.9 - build(pyproject): require triton 3.3.0+ for arm64 support * Add triton version 3.3.0 or newer to the dependencies to support arm64 architecture. - build: skip Triton on Windows since it isn't supported * Add a platform marker to the triton dependency to skip it on Windows, as triton does not support Windows. - build: configure PyTorch sources for cross-platform compatibility * macOS uses CPU-only PyTorch from pytorch-cpu index * Linux and Windows use CUDA 12.8 PyTorch from pytorch index * triton only installs on Linux with CUDA 12.8 support * Update lockfile to support multi-platform builds - fix: restrict av to <16.0.0 for Python 3.9 compatibility * Add av<16.0.0 to dependencies to maintain Python 3.9 support * Update comment to include av in the restriction list * Update uv.lock accordingly PyAV dropped Python 3.9 support in v16.0.0: https://github.com/PyAV-Org/PyAV/commit/106089447cab9dc6d3ca2d1ab7e0aeff76d7a6a4 - fix: resolve PyTorch ARM64 platform compatibility issue * Update uv.lock to properly handle aarch64 platforms for PyTorch dependencies * Add resolution markers for ARM64 Linux systems to use CPU-only PyTorch builds * Ensure CUDA builds are only used on x86_64 platforms where supported Resolves ARM64 Docker build failures by preventing uv from attempting to install CUDA PyTorch on unsupported platforms - chore: change .python-version to 3.10 --- Signed-off-by: CHEN, CHUN Signed-off-by: Jim Chen Co-authored-by: GitHub Copilot ") | 7 months agoOct 8, 2025 | | [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md "CHANGELOG.md") | [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md "CHANGELOG.md") | [bump: version 3.12.0 → 3.12.1](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/commit/2887f761a9d4a6b2d4b7d53244241b4199400f04 "bump: version 3.12.0 → 3.12.1") | last monthApr 7, 2026 | | [CLAUDE.md](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/blob/main/CLAUDE.md "CLAUDE.md") | [CLAUDE.md](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/blob/main/CLAUDE.md "CLAUDE.md") | [chore: add claude.md](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/commit/1d170c654da2e808de06bd2fa2ea8cf807bb1293 "chore: add claude.md") | 2 months agoMar 23, 2026 | | [LICENSE](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/blob/main/LICENSE "LICENSE") | [LICENSE](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/blob/main/LICENSE "LICENSE") | [BSD 2 LICENSE](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/commit/58f00339af7dcc9705ef49d97a1f40764b7cf555 "BSD 2 LICENSE") | 2 years agoJul 11, 2024 | | [MANIFEST.in](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/blob/main/MANIFEST.in "MANIFEST.in") | [MANIFEST.in](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/blob/main/MANIFEST.in "MANIFEST.in") | [fix: include assets in wheel and add Python 3.14 to CI matrix](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/commit/b4c48d021fde30ecda38a2c9340b074a8887e1ab "fix: include assets in wheel and add Python 3.14 to CI matrix The pytorch_model.bin VAD model was missing from the published wheel because MANIFEST.in referenced the old whisperx/ path and there was no explicit package-data declaration. Add [tool.setuptools.package-data] to ensure assets/* is always included. Also add Python 3.14 to the compatibility test matrix and update requires-python upper bound to <3.15. Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 ") | 2 months agoMar 23, 2026 | | [README.md](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/blob/main/README.md "README.md") | [README.md](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/blob/main/README.md "README.md") | [docs: add documentation pointer](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/commit/5d5b44945358752285ea918052f4d05092009e9b "docs: add documentation pointer") | last monthApr 10, 2026 | | [pyproject.toml](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/blob/main/pyproject.toml "pyproject.toml") | [pyproject.toml](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/blob/main/pyproject.toml "pyproject.toml") | [bump: version 3.12.0 → 3.12.1](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/commit/2887f761a9d4a6b2d4b7d53244241b4199400f04 "bump: version 3.12.0 → 3.12.1") | last monthApr 7, 2026 | | [uv.lock](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/blob/main/uv.lock "uv.lock") | [uv.lock](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/blob/main/uv.lock "uv.lock") | [bump: version 3.12.0 → 3.12.1](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/commit/2887f761a9d4a6b2d4b7d53244241b4199400f04 "bump: version 3.12.0 → 3.12.1") | last monthApr 7, 2026 | | View all files | ## Repository files navigation # whisper(ml)x [Permalink: whisper(ml)x](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx#whispermlx) Fast, accurate speech recognition on Apple Silicon — powered by [MLX](https://github.com/ml-explore/mlx). [Documentation](https://whispermlx.docs.kalebjs.dev/) A fork of [WhisperX](https://github.com/m-bain/whisperX) with the inference backend replaced by [mlx-whisper](https://github.com/ml-explore/mlx-examples/tree/main/whisper), running natively on Apple Silicon via MLX. Word-level timestamps, speaker diarization, and VAD are all retained. - ⚡️ MLX inference — runs on Apple Silicon GPU via unified memory - 🎯 Word-level timestamps via wav2vec2 forced alignment - 👥 Speaker diarization via [pyannote-audio](https://github.com/pyannote/pyannote-audio) - 🗣️ VAD preprocessing via pyannote or silero ## Installation [Permalink: Installation](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx#installation) ``` pip install whispermlx ``` Or with uv: ``` uv add whispermlx ``` ## Usage [Permalink: Usage](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx#usage) ### CLI [Permalink: CLI](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx#cli) ``` # Auto-downloads mlx-community/whisper-large-v3-mlx on first run whispermlx audio.mp3 --model large-v3 # With speaker diarization whispermlx audio.mp3 --model large-v3 --diarize --hf_token YOUR_TOKEN # Use any mlx-community model directly whispermlx audio.mp3 --model mlx-community/whisper-large-v3-turbo ``` ### Python [Permalink: Python](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx#python) ``` import whispermlx # Short name — auto-maps to mlx-community/whisper-large-v3-mlx model = whispermlx.load_model("large-v3", device="cpu") result = model.transcribe("audio.mp3") print(result["segments"]) # With alignment model_a, metadata = whispermlx.load_align_model(language_code=result["language"], device="cpu") result = whispermlx.align(result["segments"], model_a, metadata, "audio.mp3", device="cpu") # With diarization from whispermlx.diarize import DiarizationPipeline diarize_model = DiarizationPipeline(token="YOUR_HF_TOKEN", device="cpu") diarize_segments = diarize_model("audio.mp3") result = whispermlx.assign_word_speakers(diarize_segments, result) ``` ## Model Names [Permalink: Model Names](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx#model-names) Short names are automatically mapped to their `mlx-community` equivalents. Full HF repo IDs also work. | Short name | HF repo | | --- | --- | | `tiny`, `base`, `small`, `medium` | `mlx-community/whisper-{name}-mlx` | | `large-v3` | `mlx-community/whisper-large-v3-mlx` | | `large-v3-turbo` / `turbo` | `mlx-community/whisper-large-v3-turbo` | ## Speaker Diarization [Permalink: Speaker Diarization](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx#speaker-diarization) Requires a [Hugging Face access token](https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens) and acceptance of the [pyannote speaker-diarization-community-1](https://huggingface.co/pyannote/speaker-diarization-community-1) model agreement. ## Acknowledgements [Permalink: Acknowledgements](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx#acknowledgements) Built on top of [WhisperX](https://github.com/m-bain/whisperX) by Max Bain et al., [mlx-whisper](https://github.com/ml-explore/mlx-examples/tree/main/whisper), [pyannote-audio](https://github.com/pyannote/pyannote-audio), and [OpenAI Whisper](https://github.com/openai/whisper). ``` @article{bain2022whisperx, title={WhisperX: Time-Accurate Speech Transcription of Long-Form Audio}, author={Bain, Max and Huh, Jaesung and Han, Tengda and Zisserman, Andrew}, journal={INTERSPEECH 2023}, year={2023} } ``` ## About Whisper(ml)X: Automatic Speech Recognition with Word-level Timestamps (& Diarization) ### Resources [Readme](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx#readme-ov-file) ### License [BSD-2-Clause license](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx#BSD-2-Clause-1-ov-file) ### Uh oh! There was an error while loading. [Please reload this page](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx). [Activity](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/activity) ### Stars [**18**\\ stars](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/stargazers) ### Watchers [**1**\\ watching](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/watchers) ### Forks [**1**\\ fork](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/forks) [Report repository](https://github.com/contact/report-content?content_url=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2FKalebJS%2Fwhispermlx&report=KalebJS+%28user%29) ## [Releases\ 11](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/releases) [v3.12.1\\ Latest\\ \\ on Apr 7Apr 7, 2026](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/releases/tag/v3.12.1) [\+ 10 releases](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/releases) ## [Packages\ 0](https://github.com/users/KalebJS/packages?repo_name=whispermlx) No packages published ## [Contributors\ 0](https://github.com/KalebJS/whispermlx/graphs/contributors) No contributors ## Languages - Python100.0% You can’t perform that action at this time.

web.markdown:
## Use with mlx-whisper

```bash
pip install mlx-whisper
```

```python
import mlx_whisper

result = mlx_whisper.transcribe(
  speech_file,
  path_or_hf_repo="mlx-community/whisper-large-v3-mlx")
```
